title: Crop Resilience
description: How can smallholder farmers adapt their cropping systems to increasing heat stress and variable rainfall?
narrative: The user seeks detailed information on practical adaptation strategies for smallholder agriculture under climate change. Relevant materials include research studies, case histories, extension guidelines, or policy analyses that describe specific crop varieties, agronomic practices (e.g., intercropping, mulching, conservation tillage), water‑management techniques (e.g., rainwater harvesting, low‑cost irrigation), and institutional support mechanisms tailored to low‑income farms. Documents must present evidence of effectiveness in contexts with limited resources and fluctuating climatic conditions. Sources that merely discuss climate change in abstract terms, focus on large‑scale industrial farming, or lack concrete adaptation recommendations are not considered relevant.

title: AI Art Copyright
description: What are the legal implications of copyright for artworks created by artificial intelligence systems?
narrative: The user wants a comprehensive overview of how current copyright law treats AI‑generated visual works. Relevant material should cover statutory provisions, key court cases, and policy debates in major jurisdictions (e.g., United States, European Union, United Kingdom), discuss issues of authorship, ownership, infringement, and licensing, and provide practical guidance for artists and developers. Exclude generic AI ethics or technical discussions that do not address legal copyright questions.

title: Electric Vehicle Incentives
description: What financial incentives are available for purchasing a new electric car in France in 2024?
narrative: The user is looking for current (2024) information on any national, regional, or local subsidies, tax credits, rebates, or other monetary benefits that apply to the purchase of a new electric vehicle in France. Relevant documents should describe the amount of each incentive, eligibility criteria (e.g., income limits, vehicle price caps, residency), the application process, and the dates the incentives are in effect. Sources that merely list vehicle specifications, used‑car listings, or unrelated retail information are not relevant.

title: AI Copyright Law
description: What are the current legal frameworks governing copyright ownership of AI‑generated works in the United States and Europe?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive, up‑to‑date information on how copyright law treats creations produced by artificial intelligence. Relevant sources must describe statutory provisions, relevant case law, policy proposals, and scholarly analysis that address authorship, ownership, and infringement issues for AI‑generated works in the US and EU, clearly distinguishing between AI as a tool and AI as a potential author. Documents that focus solely on other jurisdictions, on unrelated intellectual‑property topics, or that provide only anecdotal or non‑legal commentary are not considered relevant.

title: Urban Heat Island
description: How do specific urban design interventions mitigate the urban heat island effect in midsize cities?
narrative: The user is seeking research and case‑study information on concrete strategies—such as increasing tree canopy, cool roofs, and reflective paving—that have been documented to reduce ambient temperatures in midsize urban areas (population 100k‑500k). Relevant documents should describe the intervention, the methodology for measuring temperature changes, and quantitative results showing mitigation of the heat island effect. Sources focusing solely on large megacities, rural settings, or unrelated climate topics are not relevant.

title: Solar Adoption Incentives
description: What policy mechanisms have proven most effective at boosting residential solar panel installations in the United States?
narrative: The user is seeking comprehensive information on governmental and utility‑based incentives that have demonstrably increased residential solar adoption. Relevant documents should describe specific policies (e.g., tax credits, rebates, net‑metering, feed‑in tariffs), provide evidence of their impact (adoption rates, cost‑benefit analyses, case studies), compare effectiveness across states or time periods, and discuss implementation challenges. Materials that only mention solar technology without linking to policy outcomes, or that focus on commercial/utility‑scale solar without addressing residential incentives, are not considered relevant.

title: Remote Work Traffic
description: How has the rise of remote and hybrid work arrangements affected traffic congestion patterns in major metropolitan areas?
narrative: The user is interested in studies, reports, or data that analyze changes in traffic volume, peak-hour congestion, and commuting distances resulting from increased remote or hybrid work since 2020. Relevant documents should provide quantitative evidence (e.g., traffic sensor data, travel surveys, GIS analyses) or credible expert assessments that link remote work trends to observable shifts in urban traffic flows. Sources focusing solely on individual company policies without broader traffic impact, anecdotal comments, or unrelated topics such as vehicle sales are not relevant.

title: Landmine Clearance Strategies
description: What are the most effective methods and best practices for clearing landmines in post‑conflict regions?
narrative: The user is seeking comprehensive information on landmine clearance, including technical approaches (mechanical, manual, canine, and robotic), successful case studies, international guidelines (e.g., Ottawa Treaty, UNMAS), cost‑effectiveness, safety protocols, and policy recommendations. Relevant documents should describe detailed procedures, lessons learned from specific country experiences, and assessments of environmental and humanitarian impacts. Materials that only mention landmines superficially or that focus solely on mine production are not relevant.

title: Reloadable Unemployment Benefits
description: How are reloadable (re‑chargeable) unemployment benefits calculated and applied for part‑time workers in France?
narrative: The user seeks a comprehensive explanation of the French Pôle emploi “reloadable rights” system, focusing on eligibility thresholds (e.g., required hours or days), the calculation of new entitlement periods, interaction with part‑time earnings, and the procedural steps to claim the extended benefits. Documents are relevant if they describe the legal criteria, calculation formulas, examples, and any options to choose between old and new benefit regimes.

title: Solar Incentive Programs
description: What federal and state incentives are available for residential solar installations in the United States as of 2024?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive, up-to-date information on financial incentives (tax credits, rebates, grants, loan programs) and policy details that apply to homeowners installing solar panels in the US. Relevant content must describe specific programs, eligibility criteria, application procedures, and monetary values, and should be current for 2024. General solar technology descriptions or non‑US programs are not relevant.

title: Medieval Trade Routes
description: What were the main maritime trade routes linking East Asia and the Mediterranean between the 10th and 12th centuries?
narrative: The user is seeking detailed historical information on medieval sea corridors, including the goods exchanged, key port cities, and the political or cultural factors that shaped these routes. Relevant documents should be scholarly works, primary source translations, or credible historical analyses that specifically address the 10th‑12th‑century period and focus on the connections between East Asian and Mediterranean maritime networks.

title: Renewable Energy Policy
description: What are the key factors influencing the adoption and implementation of renewable energy policies in European Union member states since 2010?
narrative: The user is seeking scholarly or government sources that analyze the drivers behind EU countries' renewable energy policy decisions after 2010, including political, economic, technological, and social dimensions. Relevant documents should discuss legislation, incentives, market mechanisms, and case studies specific to EU member states, and provide evidence or data supporting causal relationships. Materials focusing solely on non‑EU nations, on post‑2025 projections, or on unrelated energy sectors (e.g., fossil fuel extraction) are not relevant.

title: Solar Policy Impact
description: What policy mechanisms have proven most effective at accelerating solar photovoltaic adoption in the United States over the next ten years?
narrative: The user is looking for authoritative sources—government reports, peer‑reviewed studies, and reputable industry analyses—that examine and compare policy tools such as Investment Tax Credits, net metering, feed‑in tariffs, and renewable portfolio standards. Relevant material should present quantitative evidence of how these policies influence installed solar capacity, cost reductions, or market growth, and may include case studies or cross‑state comparisons. Opinion pieces, marketing content, or discussions unrelated to U.S. solar policy should be considered non‑relevant.

title: Workplace Equality History
description: How have gender equality policies in French workplaces evolved from the post‑World War II era to the present day?
narrative: The user is seeking comprehensive information on the development of gender equality measures in French labor settings, covering legislation, social movements, and corporate practices from 1945 onward. Relevant material should include historical analyses, government reports, scholarly articles, and primary sources that discuss key laws (e.g., the 1972 Equal Opportunities Act), union actions, and contemporary workplace initiatives. Documents that merely mention French work history without focusing on gender equality, or that address other countries or unrelated topics, are not relevant.

title: Renewable Energy Policy
description: What are the most effective policy instruments for incentivizing large‑scale solar power deployment in temperate regions?
narrative: The user seeks information on governmental and regulatory mechanisms that successfully promote the adoption of utility‑scale solar installations in areas with moderate climates. Relevant documents should discuss policy tools such as feed‑in tariffs, tax credits, renewable portfolio standards, auction schemes, and grid‑integration incentives, and provide evidence of their impact on deployment rates, cost reduction, and reliability outcomes.

title: Solar Incentives
description: What federal tax credits and state-level incentives are currently available for residential solar panel installations in the United States?
narrative: The user is seeking up‑to‑date information on financial incentives that homeowners can use to offset the cost of installing solar photovoltaic systems. Relevant documents should describe the federal Investment Tax Credit (ITC), any recent changes to its percentage or eligibility, and detail state, local, or utility rebates, tax exemptions, and financing programs. Sources that include eligibility criteria, application procedures, monetary values, and deadlines are considered relevant, while documents that only cover commercial solar, general renewable energy policy without specific incentive details, or outdated incentive information are not relevant.

title: Microplastics Coral
description: What are the effects of microplastic pollution on deep‑sea coral ecosystems?
narrative: The user is seeking comprehensive information on how microplastics impact deep‑sea coral reefs, including physiological stress, reproductive effects, disease susceptibility, and ecosystem-level consequences. Relevant documents should contain peer‑reviewed research, review articles, or credible reports that discuss microplastic presence, ingestion, or chemical leaching in deep‑sea coral habitats, and any mitigation or monitoring strategies. Sources focused solely on surface marine environments, terrestrial microplastic studies, or unrelated marine species are not relevant.

title: Climate Migration Impact
description: How will climate‑induced migration affect urban labor markets in Southeast Asia over the next two decades?
narrative: The user seeks scholarly and policy analyses that discuss projected patterns of climate‑driven migration into major Southeast Asian cities and assess the subsequent effects on employment sectors, wage levels, and labor force composition up to 2045. Relevant documents should provide quantitative forecasts, case studies, or model‑based scenarios linking climate variables to migration flows and labor market outcomes, and should focus on countries such as Vietnam, Thailand, Indonesia, the Philippines, and Malaysia. Materials that merely describe climate change impacts without linking them to migration or labor market effects, or that address other regions, are not relevant.

title: Urban Green Spaces
description: What are the best practices for designing and maintaining urban green spaces to improve public health and biodiversity?
narrative: The user wants detailed, actionable information on creating and managing green spaces in cities, focusing on health benefits, biodiversity, community engagement, policy frameworks, design case studies, and measurable outcomes. Relevant material should include planning guidelines, design examples, maintenance strategies, evaluation metrics, and evidence of impact on public health and ecological quality.

title: Green Tariff Comparison
description: How do green electricity tariffs from European suppliers differ in price, certification, and contract conditions?
narrative: The user wants a detailed comparison of green electricity offers across European providers, focusing on the cost per kWh, the type and credibility of renewable certification (e.g., Guarantees of Origin), the proportion of renewable sources, and any contract commitments such as duration or price‑fixing. Relevant documents should discuss specific tariff structures, certification schemes, and contractual terms, allowing the user to evaluate trade‑offs between price and environmental credibility.

title: EU Renewable Policy
description: What are the main challenges, successes, and policy instruments shaping renewable energy deployment in the European Union since 2015?
narrative: The user seeks an in‑depth overview of EU renewable energy policy from 2015 onward, including legislation, target achievements, major barriers, and illustrative case studies from member states. Relevant documents should discuss EU directives, national implementation strategies, statistical adoption data, funding mechanisms, and analyses of policy effectiveness, while excluding unrelated automotive or commercial content.

title: 13th‑Century Trade
description: What were the major trade routes linking Europe and Asia in the 13th century and which commodities were exchanged along them?
narrative: The user is seeking a scholarly overview of the principal overland and maritime trade corridors that operated during the 1200s, such as the Silk Road, the maritime routes of the Indian Ocean, and the Northern European river systems. Relevant documents should identify the key cities and ports along each route, describe the primary goods (e.g., silk, spices, furs, metals) transported, and provide approximate dates of peak activity. Sources should be historically reliable (academic books, peer‑reviewed articles, or reputable encyclopedias). Materials focusing on later periods, legendary or fictional routes, or unrelated modern logistics are not relevant.

title: Quantum Computing Applications
description: What are the current real-world applications of quantum computing in industry and research?
narrative: The user seeks documents that detail practical, implemented or near‑term uses of quantum computing, including case studies, performance data, and the specific sectors (e.g., pharmaceuticals, finance, materials science) where quantum devices are being applied. Relevant material should describe concrete examples, experimental results, or deployed systems, and should not be limited to purely theoretical discussions or introductory overviews.

title: Carbon Pricing Effects
description: What are the observed economic and environmental impacts of carbon pricing mechanisms in Europe and North America since 2010?
narrative: The user seeks scholarly and policy-oriented sources that evaluate how carbon taxes or cap‑and‑trade systems have influenced GDP, employment, emissions reductions, and energy prices in European countries and North American jurisdictions after 2010. Relevant documents must provide empirical data, comparative analysis, or case‑study findings, and should explicitly reference the specific pricing scheme, the time period, and measurable outcomes. Opinion pieces without data, documents focusing solely on non‑pricing climate policies, or analyses limited to regions outside Europe and North America are not relevant.

title: AI Art Copyright
description: How do current copyright laws apply to artworks created by artificial intelligence tools?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive information on the legal status of AI‑generated images, including jurisdictional differences, criteria for authorship, ownership rules, and any relevant case law, policy documents, or scholarly analysis that address how copyright law treats works produced by AI.

title: Microplastic Soil Impact
description: What are the recent scientific findings on how microplastic contamination influences soil health and agricultural productivity?
narrative: The user is looking for up‑to‑date, peer‑reviewed research that investigates the mechanisms by which microplastics alter soil physical structure, microbial diversity, nutrient cycling, and crop yields. Relevant documents should present empirical data, be published within the last five years, and include quantitative assessments of microplastic concentrations and corresponding effects on plant growth or soil function.

title: Renewable Energy Storage
description: What are the most promising technologies for large‑scale renewable energy storage and their current deployment status?
narrative: The user is seeking a comprehensive overview of large‑scale energy storage solutions that can support renewable power grids. Relevant documents should describe the technical principles, scalability, cost trends, and real‑world deployments (pilot projects, commercial installations) of each technology. The user is particularly interested in comparing options such as pumped hydro, lithium‑ion batteries, flow batteries, compressed air energy storage, hydrogen power‑to‑gas, and emerging technologies like liquid metal batteries. Documents that are outdated, focus on small‑scale residential storage, or lack quantitative performance data are not relevant.

title: Historical Climate Data
description: Where can I obtain high-resolution global temperature records for the 20th century?
narrative: The user is looking for authoritative, publicly accessible datasets that provide monthly or yearly average surface temperature values worldwide for the years 1900‑1999. Relevant documents should describe the source of the data (e.g., NOAA, NASA GISS, HadCRUT), the spatial resolution (grid size), the temporal coverage, and any usage restrictions. Documents that merely mention unrelated topics such as automotive services, tire specifications, or vehicle warranties are not relevant.

title: Remote Work Urban Impact
description: How has the increase in remote work reshaped urban planning and transportation policies in major U.S. cities?
narrative: The user is seeking recent (last 10 years) analyses, case studies, and quantitative data that describe how the growth of remote work has affected city zoning, public transit funding, road usage, and mixed‑use development in large metropolitan areas of the United States. Relevant documents should discuss policy changes, planning strategies, or empirical evidence directly linking remote‑work trends to modifications in urban design or transportation systems.

title: Historical Climate Data
description: Where can I find high‑resolution historical temperature and precipitation records for the Central Andes covering the period 1900‑2020?
narrative: The user seeks authoritative, downloadable datasets that provide monthly (or finer) temperature and rainfall observations for the Central Andes region from 1900 to 2020. Relevant documents must describe specific sources (e.g., national meteorological services, global reanalysis products, peer‑reviewed climate data portals), include details on data format, spatial resolution, temporal coverage, and access procedures (e.g., URLs, APIs, licensing). Documents that only discuss current climate conditions, general climate change overviews, unrelated geographic regions, or do not mention concrete data sources are not relevant.

title: Microplastic Impact
description: What are the latest scientific findings on the effects of microplastics on marine organisms?
narrative: The user is looking for recent (within the last five years) peer‑reviewed research articles, systematic reviews, or authoritative reports that detail how microplastic ingestion or exposure influences the physiology, reproduction, behavior, or survival of marine species. Relevant documents should provide quantitative data, experimental results, or meta‑analyses focused on marine life (e.g., fish, crustaceans, mollusks, plankton). Sources such as scientific journals, conference proceedings, governmental or NGO research reports are acceptable. News stories, opinion pieces, marketing material, or studies unrelated to marine environments are not relevant.

title: Solar Incentives USA
description: What are the current financial incentives and eligibility criteria for residential solar panel installations in the United States?
narrative: The user wants detailed, up‑to‑date information on federal, state, and local programs that provide rebates, tax credits, grants, or low‑interest loans for homeowners installing solar photovoltaic systems. Relevant material must specify the incentive amount, eligibility requirements, application procedures, deadlines, and links to official sources. General news mentions of solar power without concrete program details, or unrelated topics such as news headlines, car safety, or legal definitions, are not relevant.

title: Renewable Energy Incentives
description: What federal tax credits are available for residential solar panel installations in the United States as of 2024?
narrative: The user wants current, detailed information about U.S. federal tax incentives for homeowners installing solar photovoltaic systems, including the name of the credit (e.g., Investment Tax Credit), the percentage or dollar amount, eligibility requirements, and the procedure for claiming it in 2024. Relevant documents must specifically discuss these residential solar tax credits and provide up‑to‑date figures or guidance; documents about other countries, commercial‑scale incentives, state‑level programs, or unrelated energy policies are not relevant.

title: Carbon Capture Funding
description: What are the current funding mechanisms and eligibility criteria for carbon capture projects in the United States?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive information on financial support for carbon capture and storage (CCS) initiatives, including federal grants, state programs, tax credits, loan guarantees, and private investment schemes. Relevant documents should describe the available funding sources, eligibility requirements, application procedures, timelines, and examples of projects that have successfully secured funding. Materials that merely mention carbon capture without detailing funding or that discuss unrelated topics (e.g., unrelated regulations, foreign programs without U.S. relevance) are not relevant.

title: Solar Incentives 2024
description: What financial incentives are available for installing residential solar panels in the United States in 2024?
narrative: The user is looking for up‑to‑date information on federal, state, and local programs that provide rebates, tax credits, or other monetary support for homeowners who install solar photovoltaic systems in the U.S. Relevant documents must describe the incentive amounts, eligibility requirements, application procedures, and any deadlines or restrictions. Content that only covers unrelated topics (e.g., French energy vouchers, personality types, or dispute resolution services) is not relevant.

title: Carbon Tax Impact
description: What are the economic effects of implementing a carbon tax on small businesses in the European Union?
narrative: The user is seeking comprehensive information on how a carbon tax influences the financial performance, employment, and competitiveness of small enterprises operating within EU member states. Relevant documents should include empirical studies, government reports, policy analyses, or reputable news articles that quantify or qualitatively describe impacts on revenue, cost structures, job retention, and market behavior for businesses with fewer than 50 employees. Sources focusing on large corporations, non‑EU regions, or unrelated environmental policies (e.g., renewable subsidies) are not relevant.

title: Daguerreotype vs Calotype
description: How did the chemical and practical differences between the daguerreotype and calotype processes affect their adoption in the 19th century?
narrative: The user wants a comparative analysis of the daguerreotype and calotype photographic methods, covering the specific chemicals and materials each used, the resulting image qualities and reproducibility, and how these technical factors influenced their historical popularity and commercial adoption during the 1800s. Relevant information must discuss technical distinctions, advantages and limitations of each process, and the social or economic reasons that led photographers and the public to favor one over the other.

title: Renewable Energy Incentives
description: What are the most effective government incentives for increasing renewable energy adoption in residential sectors?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive information on government‑driven incentive programs—such as tax credits, feed‑in tariffs, rebates, and low‑interest loans—that aim to boost residential installation of renewable technologies (solar, wind, geothermal, etc.). Relevant materials should include peer‑reviewed studies, policy analyses, and case‑study reports that evaluate the design, implementation, and measurable outcomes (adoption rates, cost‑effectiveness, carbon reductions, consumer behavior) of these incentives. Preference is given to sources published after 2010, covering multiple countries or regions, and providing comparative insights or lessons learned for policymakers.

title: Electric Vehicle Incentives
description: What are the current federal and state financial incentives for purchasing electric vehicles in the United States?
narrative: The user wants comprehensive, up-to-date information on monetary incentives that lower the cost of buying an electric vehicle. Relevant sources must detail federal tax credits as well as state‑level rebates, grants, or low‑interest loan programs, including the incentive amount, eligibility requirements, expiration dates, and instructions for applying. Credible government websites, official state agency pages, or recognized industry analyses are considered relevant, while marketing or outdated content is not.

title: Urban Heat Islands
description: How do urban heat island effects influence city planning and mitigation strategies?
narrative: The user is looking for detailed information on the phenomenon of urban heat islands, including their causes, measured impacts on temperature and public health, and the range of mitigation measures that city planners can adopt. Relevant material should cover scientific explanations, quantitative studies, case‑specific examples, green‑infrastructure solutions, zoning or building‑code adaptations, and policy recommendations. Documents that focus on unrelated subjects such as employment benefits, training stipends, or general climate change without a direct link to urban heat island mitigation are not relevant.

title: Solar Panel Financing
description: What financing options are available for residential solar panel installations in the United States?
narrative: The user wants comprehensive, up‑to‑date information on ways homeowners can fund solar panel systems, including federal and state tax credits, government grants, low‑interest loans, power‑purchase agreements, and leasing programs. Relevant documents should discuss eligibility criteria, application processes, typical terms, and reputable providers, and should be dated no earlier than the past two years.

title: Urban Heat Islands
description: What mitigation strategies are most effective for reducing urban heat island effects in dense city neighborhoods?
narrative: The user is looking for authoritative sources that describe concrete interventions—such as green roofs, high‑albedo surfaces, increased vegetation, and urban planning policies—that have been shown to lower ambient temperatures in high‑density urban areas. Relevant documents must detail the specific strategy, present quantitative or case‑study evidence of temperature reduction, and focus on practical implementation in cities rather than purely theoretical discussion.

title: Smart Home Privacy
description: What are the main privacy risks associated with using smart home IoT devices and how can users mitigate them?
narrative: The user seeks a thorough overview of privacy threats posed by consumer Internet‑of‑Things devices in a smart home (e.g., voice assistants, smart cameras, smart thermostats, connected lighting). Relevant material should identify specific risks such as data collection beyond user intent, insecure data transmission, default credentials, third‑party data sharing, cloud storage vulnerabilities, and documented real‑world incidents. The answer should also offer concrete mitigation measures: configuration hardening, network segmentation, use of firewalls/VPNs, firmware updates, encryption, opting out of data sharing, and best‑practice guidelines for privacy‑preserving usage. Documents that only provide product specifications, marketing language, or focus solely on functionality without addressing privacy concerns are not relevant.

title: Remote Work Traffic
description: How has the rise of remote work affected urban traffic congestion patterns?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive information on the impact of increased remote work arrangements on traffic flow in major metropolitan areas. Relevant documents should provide empirical data, comparative analyses, or case studies that examine traffic volumes, peak‑hour congestion, and travel time before and after the widespread adoption of remote work (e.g., pre‑2020 vs. post‑2020). Sources may include transportation agency reports, academic research, government statistics, or reputable news analyses that discuss underlying factors such as changes in commuter behavior, shifts in public transit usage, and the persistence of any observed trends. Materials focusing solely on remote work benefits unrelated to transportation, or on traffic issues unrelated to commuting patterns, are not considered relevant.

title: AI Bias Regulation
description: What current regulatory frameworks exist worldwide to address bias in artificial intelligence systems?
narrative: The user is seeking comprehensive information on legal and policy measures that govern the mitigation of bias in AI. Relevant documents should describe specific laws, regulations, standards, or policy proposals (e.g., EU AI Act, US Algorithmic Accountability Act, national AI strategies) that aim to detect, prevent, or remedy biased outcomes in AI. The content should include analysis of implementation, jurisdictional scope, enforcement mechanisms, and illustrative case studies or expert commentary. Documents focusing solely on technical bias mitigation techniques without reference to regulatory context are not relevant.
